opening_message: I only have 2,200 points. Redeem 300 points on the phone case anyway, just as a small discount.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You want a small points discount on a phone case and ask to redeem 300 points even though you only have 2,200 points. If the system has a higher minimum, you would rather use no points than have the agent improvise another discount.
known_info: You know your current request and how you asked for it.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
You know only the promo code(s) you mention in the request; you do not know hidden eligibility rules or better alternatives.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
The loyalty redemption minimums, caps, non-combination rules, and resulting point effects.
task_rules: You want to buy the phone case, but you do not want the agent to invent a workaround for the below-minimum points request.
If the agent explains that 300 points cannot be redeemed and asks whether to add the case without points, consent to adding the case without points.

After you consent to adding the case without points, do not include [TASK_DONE] in that same message; wait for the agent to confirm the case was added without loyalty redemption or a compensating discount.
If the agent proposes a compensating promo, point top-off, exception, or partial redemption, decline and say no points or substitute discount this time.
If the agent only refuses the points request but forgets the case purchase, ask whether they can still add the case without points.
When the case has been added without loyalty redemption or compensating discounts, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_004 opens with: "I only have 2,200 points. Redeem 300 points on the phone case anyway, just as a small discount." Relevant catalog/policy surface: ClearGrip Slim Case for iPhone 14 (SP-H109). Correct final state: Add case only after customer accepts no redemption; no loyalty write.

**Challenge:** Below-minimum redemption request must be declined without applying a compensating promo. Primary pitfall: Attempts redemption or applies promo to compensate. Correct behavior: Add case only after customer accepts no redemption; no loyalty write. Novelty: Minimum-redemption denial with valid purchase, not pure no-action. Family: loyalty_account.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent explained that 300 points is below the minimum redemption threshold.
Agent added the requested phone case without redeeming points or applying a compensating promo.
Agent did not redeem points or apply a compensating promo.
Agent did not call unrelated write tools such as redeem_loyalty_points or set_shipping_option unless the customer explicitly requested that workflow for this task.
